signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Founded in 1959, Baskonia first started playing in Spain’s top division in the 1970s. The club’s first trophy did not arrive until 1995, when Velimir Perasovic inspired Baskonia to victory in the Spanish Cup. That era marked the elevation of the club as Baskonia reached the Saporta Cup Finals in 1994 and 1995 before finally lifting that trophy in 1996. The club, then called Tau Ceramica, reached its first Spanish League Finals in 1998 and added a second Spanish Cup in 1999. The turn of the century led to instant success in the EuroLeague as the team made the 2001 EuroLeague Finals. In 2002, Tau won the Spanish Cup and its first Spanish League championship. Stars Luis Scola, Arvydas Macijauskas and Pablo Prigioni helped Baskonia reach consecutive Final Fours from 2005 to 2008 and claim Spanish Cup triumphs in 2004 and 2006. The team remained successful domestically by winning Spanish League championships in 2008 and 2010. An exciting team containing future stars Mike James, Toko Shengelia and Davis Bertans returned to the Final Four in 2016. Three consecutive EuroLeague Playoff appearances followed, before a decade-long wait for silverware was ended in 2020 with the club’s fourth Spanish League triumph. In 2024, Baskonia returned to the EuroLeague Playoffs behind Markus Howard and Chima Moneke's leadership on the court. Loyal to its trademark character, Baskonia will be eager to fight for big wins in 2025-26.

heroMedia.title: Dubai beats Baskonia in Sarajevo to stay in playoff chase
heroMedia.summary: A 36 minute clinic gives way to late drama as Dubai survives a furious Baskonia rally to keep its postseason push alive

linkedIds.text: Dubai Basketball v Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (2026-03-12T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Dubai Basketball took a step toward squeezing into the EuroLeague postseason in the club’s maiden appearance in the premier club competition by beating Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz 100-94 for a fifth successive win, which lifted its record to 16-14.

Playing as the home team at Sarajevo’s Zetra Arena, Dubai thrived from vocal support in Bosnia-Herzegovina to grab the matchup by the scruff of the neck early on and dictate proceedings for 36 minutes in a contest that almost served up late drama.

Having led 93-73 with 4 minutes left, Dubai took its foot off the gas and allowed Baskonia to close to 98-94 before crowd favorite Dzanan Musa beat the final buzzer with a shot off the glass, finishing on 19 points and 7 boards in a high-octane team effort.

Dwayne Bacon posted a team-high 23 points, Mfiondu Kabengele racked up 17 and 6 boards, while Filip Petrusev and McKinley Wright added 11 apiece to counter a valiant individual effort by Mamadi Diakite, who amassed 27 and 12 in defeat.

Baskonia dropped to 9-22 after a sixth loss on the trot as Eugene Omoruyi (18 points) and Kobi Simmons (14) were the only other visiting players in double figures. Sharpshooting guard Markus Howard was held to 9 points on 1-of-9 shooting from deep as he struggled against aggressive defenders, which forced him to take a barrage of tough shots from the perimeter.

Although Dubai’s go-to guys and Coach Jurica Golemac were happy with the win, they were clearly displeased with the fourth-quarter meltdown, which let Baskonia back into the game in the last few minutes of what was a lopsided contest up to that point. 

“The last quarter was not good and we didn’t want to have this kind of game in the home stretch,” Bosnian guard Musa said. “In the end, what matters is that we got the win and I want to thank Sarajevo for welcoming us like this. It was an amazing atmosphere,” he added.

An unforgettable night! Hvala Sarajevo 🤍🇧🇦 pic.twitter.com/3OExnaehCm

— Dubai Basketball (@dubaibasket) March 12, 2026

Golemac was in step with one of his most influential players, having been able to breathe a huge sigh of relief after a Baskonia blitz threatened to force late drama. 

“Once again, as I keep saying, it’s a 40-minute game,” the Slovenian tactician stressed. “We were up by 20 with 4 minutes left and then we loosened up, committed some turnovers and we have to make sure it doesn’t happen again. The full gym really helped us a lot. Hopefully, we’ll be back home in Dubai as soon as possible.”

Bacon was a handful for his markers throughout and he praised Baskonia for its die-hard attitude as the visitors kept chipping away at Dubai in the last few minutes, falling just short of engineering a late twist that would have derailed the home team’s bid to reach the business end of the competition.

“The second unit did pretty well, but what we’ve got to clean up is the second-half performance, as we were up against a team that never quits,” he said. “No team is ever going to quit in the EuroLeague, so we’ve got to keep our foot on the gas until the game is over.”

linkedId.text: Dubai Basketball v Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (2026-03-12T19:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Dubai beats Baskonia in Sarajevo to stay in playoff chase
articleMetadata.description: Dubai Basketball defeated Kosner Baskonia Vitoria‑Gasteiz 100–94 at Sarajevo’s Zetra Arena to notch a fifth straight win and improve to 16–14. Dubai led by 20 late before withstanding a furious rally, sealing the result on a Dzanan Musa buzzer‑beater. Dwayne Bacon scored 23, Mfiondu Kabengele added 17, and balanced contributions overcame Mamadi Diakite’s 27‑point, 12‑rebound effort. Coach Jurica Golemac praised the atmosphere but cautioned against fourth‑quarter slippage as Dubai continues its postseason push.

heroMedia.title: Baskonia signs Dutch center Jesse Edwards
heroMedia.summary: Edwards joins Paolo Galbiati’s squad after a strong season in Australia, though ineligible for EuroLeague play

content.text:
Kosner Baskonia Vitoria‑Gasteiz has signed Dutch center Jesse Edwards for the remainder of the season. The big man will not be eligible to play in the EuroLeague this season, as the signing deadline has already passed, and will therefore represent the club exclusively in the Spanish League (ACB).

Edwards (2.13 meters, 25 years old) arrives from Australia, where he averaged 13.5 points, 8.5 rebounds and 1.1 blocks over 33 games with Melbourne United. He ranked fifth in the league in rebounding and 10th in blocks.

✍️ Jesse Edwards, nueva pieza para el juego interior de Kosner Baskonia

📰https://t.co/qH00MGNozT

Ongi etorri, Jesse! 🔵🔴 pic.twitter.com/CaYYvkj52D

— Kosner Baskonia (@Baskonia) March 10, 2026

Born and raised in Amsterdam, Edwards moved to Florida to play high school basketball at the IMG Academy. He went on to play collegiate basketball at Syracuse University and West Virginia University. Edwards signed his first professional contract with the Minnesota Timberwolves and made his professional debut in 2024 with their G League affiliate, the Iowa Wolves. He also appeared in two games for the Timberwolves during the 2024-25 season before heading to Australia.

Edwards has represented the Dutch national team and was part of the roster for EuroBasket 2022.

articleMetadata.title: Baskonia signs Dutch center Jesse Edwards
articleMetadata.description: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria Gasteiz has signed Dutch center Jesse Edwards for the rest of the season. Edwards, who averaged 13.5 points, 8.5 rebounds and 1.1 blocks with Melbourne United in the NBL, will be eligible to play only in the Spanish League due to EuroLeague registration deadlines. A product of Syracuse and West Virginia with G League and NBA experience, Edwards also represents the Dutch national team and was part of the EuroBasket 2022 roster.

heroMedia.title: Paris pulls away in second quarter against Baskonia to claim third straight road win
heroMedia.summary: Nadir Hifi and Justin Robinson combined for 40 points

linkedIds.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Paris Basketball (2026-03-06T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) surged to an 81-97 road win over [Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/baskonia-vitoria-gasteiz/bas/), extending its winning streak away from home to three consecutive victories and improving to 11-18. A second-quarter burst sparked by quick drives and sharp shooting put the visitors firmly in control, and Paris never allowed the hosts back into the game.

Coming off two crucial road wins over Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens and FC Barcelona, Paris carried its momentum into Friday's contest from the opening quarter. The visitors set the pace early, scoring easy baskets and building a 25-point second-quarter lead that put them firmly in control heading into halftime.

Paris thrived on its pace, with [Nadir Hifi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nadir-hifi/012640/) setting the tone and keeping Baskonia's defense on its heels. The sharpshooter poured in 21 points, standing out as the driving force behind the victory.

"We played well, we played together," Hifi said after the game. "We moved the ball and obviously played good defense too. We controlled the rebounds. A great game, and we're proud tonight."

content.title: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz-Paris Basketball | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Another key factor in Paris's dominance was its outside shooting. The visitors connected on 43.6% of their three-pointers, with eight different players hitting from beyond the arc. Rebounding also fueled the win, as Paris converted 21 points off second-chance opportunities.

[Justin Robinson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/justin-robinson/014008/) was another steady force for Paris, finishing with 19 points including four three-pointers, while [Jared Rhoden](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-rhoden/014178/) added 16 points and matched him from deep. [Allan Dokossi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/allan-dokossi/014010/) stood out on both ends of the floor despite scoring just 2 points, contributing 11 rebounds and 6 assists.

"I think we got a little scared at the end of the game, but from the beginning we imposed our tempo and kept playing together, passing the ball, rebounding well," said the French center after the game. "I think that's why we got the win."

In the fourth quarter, Baskonia attempted a comeback, but Paris's defense kept the hosts from closing the gap. Paris coach Francesco Tabellini was pleased with the team's defensive effort.

"In the first half we made many threes, but I think we built those shots well, and the confidence came from the defense," Tabellini said after the game. "I think we really pushed Baskonia out of their comfort zone and took away some of the certainties they have. Defensively we were really solid all evening, except for a brief lapse in the fourth quarter, but overall the guys did a very good job."

La troisième victoire de suite en EuroLeague ! 🔥🔥🔥
On se retrouve ENFIN à la maison ce mardi face à l’Olympiakos !

La billetterie est ici : https://t.co/QuNVs8ZJIK pic.twitter.com/oelpoQ848L

— Paris Basketball (@ParisBasketball) March 6, 2026

For Baskonia, [Eugene Omoruyi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/eugene-omoruyi/014207/) led the way with 17 points, while [Clement Frisch](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/clement-frisch/014026/) stood out in the third quarter with 13 points and 6 rebounds. [Timothe Luwawu-Cabarrot](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/timothe-luwawu-cabarrot/012080/) also stepped up in moments when the offense stalled, adding 13 points, but it wasn't enough.

"It's a jumble till the end," Paolo Galbiatti said after the game. "I can accept losing – they know that – if the others are better. Tonight they played a better game than us, but we weren't competitive by EuroLeague standards, and that's something we need to adjust."

linkedId.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Paris Basketball (2026-03-06T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Paris pulls away in second quarter against Baskonia to claim third straight road win
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball surged to an 81-97 road win over Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz, extending its winning streak away from home to three consecutive victories and improving to 11-18. A second-quarter burst sparked by quick drives and sharp shooting put the visitors firmly in control, and Paris never allowed the hosts back into the game.

heroMedia.title: Tadas Sedekerskis undergoes ankle surgery
heroMedia.summary: No timeline for his return to action has been announced

content.text:
Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz forward [Tadas Sedekerskis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tadas-sedekerskis/007230/) underwent surgery in Barcelona on Tuesday to address stability issues with his right ankle. 

In a statement released by the club, Baskonia declared that the procedure went as planned and the player has already begun his recovery process under the supervision of the club’s medical staff. The club also state that the surgical intervention was designed to resolve the instability that has affected the player in recent weeks.

Parte médico: Tadas Sedekerskis

📰https://t.co/LJZ3chIo4N pic.twitter.com/f0tcmUQY0u

— Kosner Baskonia (@Baskonia) March 3, 2026

No timeline for Sedekerskis's return to action has been announced, with the Lithuanian having been out of action since Round 13 of the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

Sedekerskis is in his eighth campaign in the EuroLeague, all of which have come with Baskonia.

articleMetadata.title: Tadas Sedekerskis undergoes ankle surgery
articleMetadata.description: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz forward Tadas Sedekerskis underwent surgery in Barcelona on Tuesday to address stability issues with his right ankle.

heroMedia.title: Record-breaking first half helps Valencia bounce back against Baskonia
heroMedia.summary: Pedro Martinez's team shattered several EuroLeague records at Buesa Arena

linkedIds.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Valencia Basket (2026-02-26T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/valencia-basket/pam/) was on a mission at Buesa Arena as it faced [Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/baskonia-vitoria-gasteiz/bas/) in Round 29. 

The previous weekend, Baskonia lifted the Spanish Cup trophy at Roig Arena after three big wins in as many days. Valencia suffered a heartbreaking semifinal loss to Real Madrid, but it was ready to bounce back. Baskonia fans celebrated before the game as their team presented the trophy – the club's first Spanish Cup in 17 years – to the Buesa Arena faithful.

It had been a long week for Baskonia, full of intense action and a major celebration. Even so, Valencia delivered its best performance, breaking several EuroLeague records that had stood since the beginning of the competition with an outstanding first half for the ages on its way to a 79-108 victory.

content.title: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz-Valencia Basket | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Valencia led 36-68 at halftime. The 32-point margin set a new EuroLeague record for a road team at halftime. FC Barcelona held the previous record with a 31-point halftime lead (19-50) against Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul on February 13, 2013. Barcelona went on to win that game 60-99. But there was more.

Valencia broke two additional records in its iconic first half. Its 68 points were the most ever scored by a team in a EuroLeague first half. [Neal Sako](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/neal-sako/010545/) capped the historic effort with a put-back dunk seconds before the break. Virtus Bologna previously held the record with 66 first-half points against Cibona Zagreb on October 25, 2000. Virtus won that game 116–88 and later became the original EuroLeague champion, defeating Baskonia 3-2 in the five-game finals.

It was also the highest scoring total by a road team in any half in EuroLeague history. Only Real Madrid has scored more points in any half – 70 in the second half of a 116–94 home victory over Ovarense Aerosoles on December 20, 2000. That remains one of the oldest team records in competition history.

"I think we needed this win and we worked for it," [Jean Montero](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jean-montero/010042/) said after the game. "We stayed true to our game, our philosophy – running, playing aggressive defense, and obviously shooting a lot of threes."

Valencia did not slow down after halftime, extending its lead to 58–93 after three quarters. It marked the fifth-highest scoring total through three quarters in EuroLeague history and the second-highest by a road team. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens scored 94 points in the first 30 minutes of an 83–123 road victory over Chorale Roanne on November 21, 2007.

19 en @EuroLeague, familia 🧡

El jueves que viene volvemos, en casa, en un partido muy importante para seguir mirando arriba en la máxima competición europea.

Tocará empujar como vosotros sabéis 🔥

Bona nit 🧡 pic.twitter.com/0UID3poIDS

— Valencia Basket Club (@valenciabasket) February 26, 2026

Baskonia trimmed the deficit in the fourth quarter but could not prevent Valencia from setting a new club scoring record in the competition with 108 points. The previous record was 107 in a home win over Anadolu Efes Istanbul on February 18, 2004. Montero led the way with 20 points, 7 rebounds and 6 assists. [Braxton Key](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/braxton-key/014168/) set new career highs with 14 points and a PIR of 20. All 12 Valencia players scored at least 4 points.

"We had a heartbreaker against Real Madrid. We practiced hard this week, ready to get back to playing, ready to get back to competition. So it was a good win for us, for sure," Key said after the game. 

"We've got to get every win we can right now. We knew it was going to be a tough game – they just won Copa – so we knew coming in we had to be focused and ready to go. We’ve got to stack wins right now. But it starts each day in practice. Day by day, we’ll keep it going and hopefully carry that momentum into the playoffs."

Valencia plays three of its next four games at home, beginning with its Round 30 showdown against Zalgiris Kaunas on Thursday, March 5.

"It's a very balanced competition," coach Pedro Martinez said after the game. "We're in a good position now, but nine games is a lot. If we lose two in a row, we could drop six or seven spots. We have to stay calm and be ready for the next game."

linkedId.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Valencia Basket (2026-02-26T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Record-breaking first half helps Valencia bounce back against Baskonia
articleMetadata.description: Valencia Basket was on a mission at Buesa Arena as it faced Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz in Round 29. Valencia delivered its best performance, breaking several EuroLeague records that had stood since the beginning of the competition with an outstanding first half for the ages on its way to a 79-108 victory.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Baskonia stuns Real Madrid to lift Spanish Copa del Rey
heroMedia.summary: Trent Forrest named MVP as Luwawu Cabarrot and Omoruyi headline an 89-100 upset in Valencia

content.text:
Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz captured the 2026 Spanish Copa del Rey with an 89-100 victory over Real Madrid on Sunday at Roig Arena in Valencia, ending a 17‑year wait to reclaim the trophy. Against the odds, Coach Paolo Galbiati’s team weathered an early Madrid blitz and finished with a late flourish to secure the club’s seventh cup.

Trent Forrest was named MVP of the Copa del Rey after authoring a near triple‑double in the final with 22 points – including 7 decisive points in the final 2 minutes – plus 11 assists and 9 rebounds for a PIR of 38. Timothé Luwawu‑Cabarrot scored 28 points,  Eugene Omoruyi added 23 and Mamadi Diakite added 9 points and 5 blocks to anchor the paint. For Madrid, Andres Feliz and Mario Hezonja each scored 15 points and Facundo Campazzo tallied 14 in defeat.

Madrid burst from the blocks like it had in the semifinal, springing to a 13-2 start behind threes from Sergio Llull, Campazzo and Hezonja. Baskonia steadied through Luwawu‑Cabarrot’s relentless drives and Matteo Spagnolo’s spark to nudge in front, only for Feliz and Alex Len to power a 14-0 response that restored *Los Blancos’* control at 40-30 Even so, Baskonia’s core of Luwawu‑Cabarrot, Forrest and Omoruyi trimmed the damage before the break, sending the game to halftime with Madrid narrowly ahead, 52-47.

Forrest and Luwawu‑Cabarrot kept their foot on the gas after the break, drawing Baskonia level at 57-57 and forcing Madrid to regroup. The fourth quarter opened with TLC already on 28 and carrying the offense, but once Madrid tightened the screws on him — and he fouled out with 4 minutes to play — Omoruyi and Forrest seized the moment to push Baskonia in front, 83-85, and build a two‑possession cushion. A late Hezonja triple briefly raised Los Blancos’ hopes, yet Markus Howard answered cold‑bloodedly, and Baskonia crossed the finish line with authority to complete a statement cup run.

articleMetadata.title: Baskonia stuns Real Madrid to lift Spanish Copa del Rey
articleMetadata.description: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria‑Gasteiz captured the 2026 Copa del Rey with a 100–89 victory over Real Madrid at Roig Arena in Valencia, claiming the club’s seventh cup and its first in 17 years. Trent Forrest earned MVP honors after posting 22 points, 11 assists and 9 rebounds, while Timothé Luwawu‑Cabarrot scored 28 and Eugene Omoruyi added 23. Mamadi Diakite contributed key defensive plays and Markus Howard delivered late as Baskonia overcame Madrid’s hot start and sealed a historic comeback win.
